sourceExcerpt: 21 CFR 211.68(b) — Appropriate controls shall be exercised over computer or related systems to assure that changes in master production and control records or other records are instituted only by authorized personnel.
suggestedObligationText: Implement access controls for computerized systems used in production and control record management
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: Only authorized people can change production and control records in computer systems
confidenceExplanation: Direct regulatory citation with clear obligation text. High confidence based on unambiguous language.
sourceReference: 21 CFR Part 211 — Current Good Manufacturing Practice for Finished Pharmaceuticals

sourceExcerpt: ICH Q10, Section 3.2 — Management responsibilities include ensuring that an effective pharmaceutical quality system is established, implemented and maintained.
suggestedObligationText: Senior management must establish, implement, and maintain an effective pharmaceutical quality system
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: Company leadership is responsible for having a working quality system
confidenceExplanation: ICH guideline language. Clear management obligation. Slight ambiguity in 'effective' definition reduces confidence.

sourceExcerpt: 21 CFR 211.194(a) — Laboratory records shall include a statement of each method used in the testing of the sample.
suggestedObligationText: Laboratory test methods must be documented in test records
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: Every lab test needs to say which method was used
confidenceExplanation: Clear regulatory text but interpretation scope was too narrow — did not capture full record retention context.
reviewerNotes: Interpretation too narrow. Missing record retention requirements from 211.194(a)(2)-(8). Re-extract with broader context window.
sourceReference: 21 CFR Part 211 — Current Good Manufacturing Practice for Finished Pharmaceuticals

sourceExcerpt: EU GMP Annex 11, Clause 7.1 — Data should be secured by both physical and electronic means against damage. Stored data should be checked for accessibility, readability and accuracy.
suggestedObligationText: Ensure data integrity through physical and electronic security, with periodic verification of stored data
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: Protect data from damage and regularly check that stored data can still be accessed and read correctly
confidenceExplanation: Cross-reference between EU GMP Annex 11 and 21 CFR Part 11 data integrity requirements. Strong thematic alignment.

sourceExcerpt: ICH Q7, Section 6.14 — In-process sampling should be conducted using procedures designed to prevent contamination of the sampled material and other intermediates or APIs.
suggestedObligationText: In-process sampling procedures must prevent contamination of sampled material
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: When taking samples during manufacturing, the sampling method cannot contaminate what's being tested or other materials
confidenceExplanation: Clear regulatory requirement. High confidence. Expired due to source record version update.
sourceReference: ICH Q7 — Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients

sourceExcerpt: 21 CFR 211.22(a) — There shall be a quality control unit that shall have the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging material, labeling, and drug products.
suggestedObligationText: Establish a quality control unit with authority to approve or reject all materials and products
suggestedPlainEnglishInterpretation: A dedicated quality team must have the power to accept or reject everything used in or produced during manufacturing
confidenceExplanation: Foundational GMP requirement with explicit regulatory language. Very high confidence.
sourceReference: 21 CFR Part 211 — Current Good Manufacturing Practice for Finished Pharmaceuticals
